title: cordia oncocalyx
intro: Cordia oncocalyx, also known as the 'Kadupul', is a tropical flowering plant native to Sri Lanka and parts of India. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds that help to soothe various health issues. The health benefits of Cordia oncocalyx include its ability to lower blood pressure, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems, making it a valuable herb for promoting overall well-being. Herbal preparations of Cordia oncocalyx can be made by infusing its leaves in hot water or combining them with other herbs to create teas, infusions, and decoctions that can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
